question_title: At Google Cloud Next ’26, will Google announce a new named TPU generation not publicly announced before April 2, 2026?
summary: Google TPU v8 (codenames Sunfish/Zebrafish) is confirmed in active development by multiple industry analysts (SemiAnalysis, JY Industry Insights), with a projected H2 2026 launch. The timing would fit perfectly with a Cloud Next 2026 announcement, matching the Ironwood pattern (announced April 2025, GA Nov 2025). The Broadcom deal for "future generations of TPUs" signed April 6 creates PR momentum. However, there is NO direct evidence Google plans to announce a new TPU generation at this specific event — no leaked keynote agenda, no pre-event teasers, and the only preview article mentioning hardware references "Updates on 7th Gen TPUs." Ironwood only went GA ~5 months ago, and Google could use I/O 2026 (typically May) instead. The historical base rate of new TPU at Cloud Next is 2/3 but from only 3 data points. Status quo persistence favors NO when there's no concrete announcement evidence.

factors.description: TPU v8 (Sunfish/Zebrafish) is confirmed in active development by multiple industry analysts (SemiAnalysis, JY Industry Insights), with H2 2026 projected launch. A chip exists that could be announced.
Timing alignment: TPU v8 projected H2 2026 launch matches Ironwood pattern (announced April 2025, GA Nov 2025). An April 2026 announcement at Cloud Next would follow the same ~6-month lead time.
Broadcom deal signed April 6, 2026 for 'future generations of TPUs' through 2031 - creates PR momentum just 16 days before Cloud Next. Companies often coordinate major deals with flagship events.
Cloud Next has been venue for 2 of last 3 new TPU announcements (v5e in 2023, Ironwood in 2025). Cloud Next comes before I/O in 2026, making it the first major event opportunity.

No direct evidence of planned announcement: no leaked keynote agenda, no pre-event teasers from Google about new TPU, no insider reports. GW Apps preview article only mentions 'Updates on 7th Gen TPUs'.
Ironwood only went GA ~5 months ago (Nov 2025). Announcing a successor could cannibalize Ironwood adoption. 2024 Cloud Next similarly focused on existing TPU GA rather than new generation.

Status quo persistence: most anticipated announcements don't happen on schedule. Without concrete evidence (pre-event hype, leaked sessions, official previews), the default is no dramatic new announcement.
Google could announce at Google I/O 2026 (typically May), Hot Chips, or other venue instead. Trillium was announced at I/O 2024, not Cloud Next. The question is specifically about Cloud Next 2026.

reasoning:
**Forecast: 30% probability (logit: -0.85)**

**Key reasoning:**

- **TPU v8 exists and is in development** — Industry sources (SemiAnalysis, JY Industry Insights) confirm codenames Sunfish (TPUv8AX) and Zebrafish (TPUv8X) with a projected H2 2026 launch
- **Timing is compatible** — The Ironwood precedent (announced April 2025, GA Nov 2025) shows Google is willing to preview TPUs ~6 months before GA, which fits a potential April 2026 announcement
- **Broadcom deal creates momentum** — The April 6 deal for "future generations of TPUs" through 2031 was signed just 16 days before Cloud Next opens
- **But no direct evidence** — No leaked keynote agenda, no pre-event teasers, and the one event preview article only mentions "Updates on 7th Gen TPUs"
- **Alternative venues exist** — Google I/O (typically May) has historically been a common TPU announcement venue (Trillium was announced there in 2024)
- **Recency of Ironwood GA** — Ironwood went GA only ~5 months ago; 2024 showed Google will use Cloud Next for incremental updates rather than new gen reveals

resolution_criteria: This question resolves as **Yes** if Google introduces a newly named TPU generation or successor family beyond Ironwood; GA, pricing, or availability updates for existing TPU families do not count. Resolution source URLs: https://www.googlecloudevents.com/next-vegas and https://cloud.google.com/blog/topics/google-cloud-next or  [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions)
